meta.note: Real market-economics reference points snapshotted from The AI Ledger's published model and gateway tracking. These are the proprietary, market-level numbers Hepburn maintains in TAIL; the Enterprise AI Ledger uses them to benchmark a company's AI economics — an economic view a generic adviser cannot reproduce without this data.

fx.note: Illustrative AUD/USD for converting TAIL's USD market figures to Meridian's AUD.

tokenEconomics.basis: Blended realised price across inference gateways the AI Ledger tracks (Portkey US$0.68/M over 1.9T tokens; OpenRouter US$0.80/M over 0.28T tokens). Includes cheap open-weight traffic, so frontier-model rates sit well above this.
tokenEconomics.frontierNote: The AI Ledger's per-model price tracking shows frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini) at materially higher rates — Meridian's premium-model mix runs above the blended gateway rate.

subsidyEconomics.basis: The AI Ledger reconciles industry customer revenue (US$21.1B) against true system cost (US$58.8B): customers fund only ~36% of the real cost of the AI they use; the gap (US$35.3B) is VC subsidy — US$1.67 for every US$1 of revenue. Today's token, inference and seat prices are therefore subsidised.
subsidyEconomics.implication: A prudent CFO should stress-test AI unit costs against price normalisation. If vendor pricing moves toward cost-recovery, the same usage costs up to 2.78x today's price. Only the AI Ledger quantifies this gap.

marketScale.basis: Cumulative hyperscaler + AI-lab AI capex US$766B against 406.5 trillion tokens of measured inference and US$23.5B gross customer revenue — the scale the unit economics above are drawn from.
